channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Woman arrested after explosives discovered at Tesla dealership
item.description: Forty-year-old Lucy Grace Nelson was charged with bringing explosives to a Colorado Tesla dealership after a series of incidents at the business.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israelis bid farewell to Shiri Bibas and her 2 young sons killed in captivity in Gaza
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Holding flags, orange balloons and signs saying “forgive us,” tens of thousands of Israelis lined highways as the bodies of a mother and her two young sons, killed in captivity in the Gaza Strip, were taken for burial on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pentagon orders new purge of social media sites to dump diversity, inclusion mentions by March 5
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Building lethality in the military may be the buzzword for the new Trump administration, but busywork and paperwork have become the reality at the Pentagon, as service members and civilian workers are facing a broad mandate to purge all of the department's social media sites and un
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and TARA COPP, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South Carolina man set to face a firing squad seeks postponement, questioning execution procedures
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina man who is scheduled to die next week by firing squad is again asking that his execution be postponed and prison officials release more information about the state's lethal injection drug and procedures.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hidden camera found in Rhode Island College bathroom
item.description: A student was using the second-floor bathroom when he noticed what appeared to be a lens tucked next to a sprinkler in the ceiling.
item.dc:creator: Tim White, NewsNation

item.title: SEE IT: New York police save horse from icy pond in dramatic rescue
item.description: A horse that fell into an icy pond was rescued by police officers and neighbors after the animal struggled to get out of the cold water, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas lottery drawings that paid out big jackpots are the focus of widening investigatons
item.description: Two of the biggest lottery jackpots in Texas history are under investigation over claims that the winners may have benefitted from more than good luck.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Lathan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 3 councilmen in South Carolina's third-largest city charged in bribery and kickback scheme
item.description: Three members of the City Council in South Carolina's third-largest city accepted kickbacks and bribes to give out grant money and rezone land or lied about what they knew, federal prosecutors said Wednesday, Charges against North Charleston councilmen Jerome Heyward, Sandino Moses and Mike A.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ryan Reynolds' image takes hit amid Lively-Baldoni lawsuit; costars speak out
item.description: "Would I work with him again? No, I would not work with him again," said Reynolds' "Deadpool" co-star.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: The Trump administration sets the stage for large-scale federal worker layoffs in a new memo
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. government is facing a generational realignment as President Donald Trump directs federal agencies to develop plans for eliminating employee positions and consolidating programs.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Immigration officials say everyone living in the US illegally must register: What to know
item.description: Federal immigration law has long required that people living illegally in the U.S. register with the government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Democrat mayor praises his handling of migrant crisis as controversial new shelter opens
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams has praised his administration’s handling of the city's migrant crisis days after a new 2,200-capacity all-male shelter opened in the Bronx.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former NY detective moonlighted as Bonanno crime family soldier, helped fuel mini-mob war, feds allege
item.description: Hector Rosario, a former Nassau County detective, ‘sold himself’ to major crime clan Bonanno. He allegedly helped tip off members about multiple investigations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Search underway for Colorado man in Black Canyon of the Gunnison National Park
item.description: Officials are searching for Jordan Marsters, a 31-year-old who disappeared two weeks ago, in Black Canyon of the Gunnison National Park in Colorado.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Egg prices could jump 41% this year, USDA says, as Trump's bird flu plan is unveiled
item.description: The Trump administration believes a renewed emphasis on biosecurity can help in the battle against bird flu and ease egg prices.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk And Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Agriculture secretary outlines plan to lower egg prices
item.description: “The Agriculture Department will invest up to $1 billion to curb this crisis and make eggs affordable again," Rollins wrote in an op-ed.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NFLPA report cards: Dolphins, Vikings finish at the top for second straight year
item.description: The Miami Dolphins are the top-ranked team, followed by the Minnesota Vikings, for the second consecutive season in the NFL Players Association report card.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Michelle Trachtenberg, 'Gossip Girl' and 'Buffy' actress, dead at 39
item.description: Actress Michelle Trachtenberg has died, police sources confirmed to multiple news outlets on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: After a month of Trump's pro-oil and gas moves, Dems target his energy emergency
item.description: President Donald Trump pledged a U-turn away from the Biden administration's commitment to fighting climate change and promoting clean energy.
item.dc:creator: Michael Phillis And Jennifer Mcdermott, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: American Airlines flight discontinues landing to avoid departing plane in Virginia
item.description: The maneuver occurred less than two hours before another plane attempting to land at Chicago’s Midway Airport was forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Prominent Colorado developer assassinated at luxury Belize home: report
item.description: A real estate developer from Denver, Colorado was fatally shot in Belize and police say it could have been the result of a professional hit by a disgruntled business associate, according to local reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams claims prosecutorial misconduct, asking judge to dismiss criminal case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams asked a federal judge to toss out the corruption case against him Wednesday, alleging prosecutorial misconduct, even as the Justice Department seeks dismissal of the charges on the Democrat's behalf.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: RFK Jr. targets childhood psychiatric drugs; doctors push back
item.description: Should Kennedy seek to limit access to these medications, there are some avenues available to him in his new role.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: WATCH: Officers cut open man's 'narco wig' at Colombian airport to reveal cocaine
item.description: He may have been using his head, but a 40-year-old passenger wasn't able to get past Colombian airport security with a cocaine-stuffed "narco wig," police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4

item.title: iPhone bug that could suggest replacing 'racist' with 'Trump' is getting fixed
item.description: Apple is fixing a bug within the dictation feature on some iPhones that briefly suggests the word “Trump” when a word with an R consonant is spoken, including "racist.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Student loan borrowers face abrupt 180 as GOP budget plans threaten to raise payments
item.description: Student loan borrowers could see their payments increase under budget plans currently under discussion by House Republicans, a violent about-face after years of working with the Biden administration to lower payments and secure debt relief.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, ABC 4

item.title: Judge blocks Trump immigration policy allowing arrests in churches for some religious groups
item.description: A federal judge on Monday blocked immigration agents from conducting enforcement operations in houses of worship for Quakers and a handful of other religious groups.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Antivirus software company ordered to pay customer $16.5M: How to file a claim
item.description: Millions of American consumers who bought antivirus software from Avast may be eligible to receive a portion of a $16.5 million settlement, the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Mississippi city drops lawsuit over newspaper editorial that judge ordered removed
item.description: A Mississippi city dropped its lawsuit Monday against a newspaper that had its editorial criticizing local leaders removed by a judge in a case that sparked widespread outrage from First Amendment advocates.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Coast Guard rescues 3 boaters stuck at sea for 9 hours after their boat capsizes
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Coast Guard crews rescued three boaters in Captiva Island, Florida, after their boat flipped over and they were stuck at sea for nine hours. A family member reported concerns at 1 a.m. Sunday after not hearing from the three boaters since 8 p.m. Saturday.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge declines to restore AP access to White House amid 'Gulf of America' dispute
item.description: A federal judge on Monday declined to restore The Associated Press’s access to certain spaces open to the media at the White House, including the Oval Office amid the Trump administration’s battle against the wire service over its refusal to use “Gulf of America” in its popular stylebook. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Trump defends Musk’s ‘genius’ email to federal workers
item.description: President Trump on Monday defended tech billionaire Elon Musk after pushback over his email demanding federal workers list five accomplishments from the past week and in doing so, raising the of risk losing their jobs. “There was a lot of genius in sending it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Starbucks lays off 1,100 corporate employees as coffee chain streamlines
item.description: Starbucks plans to lay off 1,100 corporate employees globally as new Chairman and CEO Brian Niccol streamlines operations. In a letter to employees released Monday, Niccol said the company will inform employees who are being laid off by midday Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Angry Democratic donors turn off the flow of money
item.description: Democrats are anxious to rebuild their party on the heels of President Donald Trump’s victory in November. But they have a major problem as they try to refashion their brand: The money isn’t there.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, Fox 5

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war enters third year
item.description: As the war in Ukraine enters its third year, the United States is ramping up efforts to broker a peace deal, but sharp divisions are emerging over how to approach Russia’s role in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, Fox 5

item.title: Federal workers confront mass confusion as Musk's deadline looms
item.description: Confusion and chaos loom as hundreds of thousands of federal employees begin their workweek facing a deadline from Elon Musk to explain their recent accomplishments or risk losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The Trump administration is firing 2,000 USAID workers and putting thousands of others on leave
item.description: The Trump administration said Sunday that it was placing all but a fraction of staffers at the U.S. Agency for International Development on leave worldwide and eliminating 2,000 U.S.-based staff positions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
